title: Lion Gate, Mycenae
description: Test your knowledge on this object

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is NOT a Bronze Age Aegean culture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Cyclopean, taken from the legendary Cyclops, is the term used to describe the large stones that fortify the buildings at Mycenae. Cyclopean is not a Bronze age Aegean culture.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-lion-gate)**

assessment_items.question_content: **The triangular space of the *Lion Gate* is formed by the corbelling of the  [[☃ dropdown 1]] and is supported by the post and lintel construction of the  [[☃ dropdown 2]].**
hints: Post and lintel construction relies on a horizontal element laid flat across the top of two supporting vertical elements.

In this example, the door is a post and lintel structure supporting the corbelled wall above.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-lion-gate)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which detail of the *Lion Gate* may reflect the influence of Minoan culture?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1fa26260d2b239f8e4e00fa79f0bb1ab.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The column in the *Lion Gate* recalls Minoan architectural style in its capital and the tapering of the shaft.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-lion-gate)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What mythological creature inspired the name describing the large stone masonry used by the Mycenaeans? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The term Cyclopean Masonry, based on the mythic giant Cyclops, is used to describe the Mycenaen's large stone construction technique.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-lion-gate)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which has been proposed about this ancient sculpture? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1fa26260d2b239f8e4e00fa79f0bb1ab.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There is no evidence that gold lion heads were originally attached to the bodies of the stone lions.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/aegean-art/v/mycenae-lion-gate)**

choices.content: The figures originally had bird heads.
The animals are humanized in the way they are depicted.
The image suggests the merging of two cultures.
Gold lion heads shown in profile were originally attached to the stone relief.
